MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-07-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. FRANK GERALD ADAM, TEL. NO. (650) 738-1462 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID J. FORAN, TEL. NO. 775-0891 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS TONY CHAU and PAULINE CHAU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The Court denies summary judgment and grants summary adjudication in part and denied in part. The Court denies summary adjudication on the first cause of action for common law invasion of privacy and the second cause of action for constructive intrusion/invasion of privacy under CC 1708.8, and the fourth case of action for negligence per se. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the negligence and nuisance causes of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's conduct is unreasonable and a nuisance within the meaning of CC 3479. The Court grants summary adjudication on the declaratory relief cause of action under CCP 1061 because it is duplicative and unnecessary. The Court denies summary adjudication on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's conduct is outrageous. Lastly, the trial court will determine whether plaintiff is entitled to permanent injunctive relief. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312) Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/MJM

Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. FRANK GERALD ADAM, TEL. NO. (650) 738-1462 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID J. FORAN, TEL. NO. 775-0891 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PLAINTIFF SUSAN WOLFF's Motion For A Preliminary Injunction is Granted in part. Defendant must turn off thelight or obtain a lower watt/less bright light and motion detector that triggers the light only when there is movement. Plaintiff shall post a $1,000 bond. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint

RULING: The court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SUSAN WOLFF's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
